mnemonics.title: PMFI Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: Remember the four major tasks as PMFI: Process management, Memory management, File management, I/O device management. Think 'PMFI - Please Manage Files In' (the system).

mnemonics.title: OS as Resource Manager
mnemonics.content: OS manages: CPU (Process), Memory, Files (Storage), and Devices (I/O). Remember: 'Computers Make Fast Devices' - CPU, Memory, File, Device.

acronyms.usage: The software that manages computer hardware and provides services to applications.
Primary memory that can be accessed randomly; managed by OS Memory Management.
The main processor managed by OS Process Management for task execution.
Operations for transferring data between the computer and external devices.
Memory management technique using disk to extend RAM.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a table with four columns: Process, Memory, File, I/O. Draw icons for each - a running person for Process, a grid for Memory, a folder for File, and a keyboard/printer for I/O.
Draw layers: Bottom = Hardware, Middle = OS, Top = Applications. Show arrows from Apps to OS to Hardware representing the interface role.
Picture RAM as blocks, with OS allocating/deallocating blocks to processes. Show 'protection walls' between process memory spaces.

analogies.analogy: Like a hospital administrator who manages staff (CPU), patient records (files), medical supplies (memory), and equipment (I/O devices) to keep the hospital running smoothly.
Like a project manager who creates task lists (processes), schedules them (scheduling), and ensures they complete without conflicts.
Like a hotel manager who allocates rooms to guests (processes), tracks availability, and ensures guests don't enter others' rooms (protection).
